obligations.description: Catalog metadata update during migration (R30-R35) depends on catalog-replication (WD-06, not yet specified). Requirements R30-R35 must be revisited when catalog-replication is specified.

F02.R33 VIOLATED: readKeyIndexV2 does not validate blockIndex in [0, blockCount) or intraBlockOffset >= 0 at key-index read time. Corrupt values surface as IndexOutOfBoundsException (from CompressionMap.entry) or AssertionError/AIOOBE (from EntryCodec.decode assert), not the descriptive IOException R33 requires. Related to R40 PARTIAL.
obligations.resolved_by: spec-verify (F02 v2: code fix added bounds validation in readKeyIndexV2 + replaced assert with runtime check in EntryCodec.decode)

obligations.description: F02.R39g VIOLATED: when dictionaryMaxBufferBytes is exceeded, TrieSSTableWriter abandons dictionary training and falls back to plain-codec compression (dictBufferAbandoned=true) instead of failing with IOException as the spec requires. Either the writer should throw IOException or R39g should be amended to codify the graceful-fallback behavior.
obligations.resolved_by: spec-verify (F02 v2: R39g amended to codify graceful-fallback as the correct behavior)

obligations.description: F15.R27 VIOLATED: JsonParser uses mutual recursion (parseValue → parseObject/parseArray → parseValue) for nested structure traversal, despite the spec requiring "must not use recursion for depth traversal." The code comment at JsonParser.java:153-156 explicitly acknowledges the deviation, accepting bounded recursion (capped by R52 at maxDepth=4096) in exchange for simpler code. Resolution path: either rewrite as an explicit stack-based state machine in Materializer, or amend R27 to permit bounded mutual recursion with an explicit maxDepth cap.
obligations.resolved_by: spec-verify (F15 v3: Materializer rewritten to use explicit Deque<Frame> stack; parseObject/parseArray/currentDepth removed)

obligations.description: F10.R79-R84 VIOLATED (plus R5 PARTIAL): FullTextFieldIndex is a stub — all operations (onInsert, onUpdate, onDelete, lookup, supports, definition, close) throw UnsupportedOperationException. IndexRegistry.validate correctly enforces STRING/BoundedString field-type constraints for FULL_TEXT indices, so IndexDefinitions can be registered, but any table with a FULL_TEXT index fails on first write. Resolution requires wiring LsmFullTextIndex from jlsm-indexing through the jlsm-core interface boundary.
obligations.resolved_by: cross-module-integration WD-01: FullTextIndex.Factory SPI + LsmFullTextIndexFactory + FullTextFieldIndex adapter

obligations.description: F10.R85-R90 VIOLATED (plus R6 PARTIAL): VectorFieldIndex is a stub — onInsert/onUpdate/onDelete are no-ops, supports() always returns false, lookup() throws UnsupportedOperationException. IndexRegistry.validate correctly enforces VectorType field-type constraints for VECTOR indices. Silent no-op mutations are especially hazardous because tables with VECTOR indices accept writes that are never actually indexed. Resolution requires wiring LsmVectorIndex (IvfFlat/Hnsw) from jlsm-vector through the jlsm-core interface boundary.
obligations.resolved_by: cross-module-integration WD-02: VectorIndex.Factory nested SPI in jlsm-core keyed on (tableName, fieldName, dimensions, precision, similarityFunction); jlsm-vector supplies LsmVectorIndexFactory; VectorFieldIndex adapts SecondaryIndex mutations to VectorIndex.index/remove and VectorNearest lookup to VectorIndex.search; silent no-op writes eliminated — missing factory is now a fail-fast IllegalArgumentException at table build().

obligations.description: F41 (Encryption Lifecycle) has ~70 requirements covering per-field HKDF-SHA256 key derivation, KEK/DEK envelope hierarchy, key registry with atomic updates and CRC-32C, 4-byte DEK version tag on ciphertext, compaction-driven re-encryption, schema version tag in SSTable footer, leakageProfile() method, power-of-2 response padding, KEK rotation with dual-read window, and online encryption migration. Discovered during F03 spec-verify (2026-04-17): the only implementation is a single comment in DataTransformationAdversarialTest acknowledging GCM key truncation lacks HKDF. F41 was APPROVED after three adversarial review passes but never built. State demoted to DRAFT until implementation exists. Partial work landed under F03 Phase 5: MAC wrapping for OPE/DCPE (F41.R22 amendment), DCPE serializer integration, finally-block key zeroing. Full F41 build remains open. When implemented, the on-disk ciphertext format will need a migration pass to add the 4-byte DEK version tag prefix; existing data written under the interim format must be re-encrypted via the compaction-driven mechanism (F41.R25, R38).
obligations.blocked_by: multi-week implementation project spanning crypto, storage, engine, and manifest modules

obligations.description: F04.R34-R38 (AMENDED): full RAPID consensus is not implemented. RapidMembership ships as a Rapid-inspired unilateral protocol (each node applies suspicions/view changes independently). Deferred components: multi-process cut detection, quorum-based consensus rounds (default 75%) with bounded timeout, observer-agreement broadcast before suspicion application, and self-refutation via incarnation bump. The Member.incarnation field is already in place and DEAD->ALIVE rejoin increments it (R13), but a live member receiving a suspicion about itself does not today refute by broadcasting a higher-incarnation alive announcement.
obligations.resolved_by: f04-obligation-resolution WD-04: full RAPID consensus — ExpanderGraphOverlay monitoring, ConsensusCoordinator with observer-quorum voting, self-refutation via incarnation bump, configurable round timeout

obligations.description: F04.R35 (AMENDED): protocolTick pings every ALIVE member every protocol period rather than distributing monitoring across a bounded expander-graph overlay. Scales linearly with cluster size. For clusters over ~100 nodes an expander graph with configurable degree must be constructed and maintained under churn. Blocked on consensus design because consensus over suspicions presumes a known observer set per monitored peer.
obligations.resolved_by: f04-obligation-resolution WD-04: full RAPID consensus — ExpanderGraphOverlay monitoring, ConsensusCoordinator with observer-quorum voting, self-refutation via incarnation bump, configurable round timeout

obligations.description: F04.R41-R43 (AMENDED): split-brain handling not implemented. hasQuorum exposes quorum status but the engine does not transition to read-only on quorum loss, does not proactively retry seed contact, and does not merge conflicting views on partition heal. R92 prevents epoch regression but true reconciliation (higher incarnation wins; equal incarnations resolve by severity DEAD > SUSPECTED > ALIVE) is future work.
obligations.resolved_by: f04-obligation-resolution WD-05: ClusterOperationalMode (NORMAL/READ_ONLY), QuorumLostException, quorum-monitoring in ClusteredEngine.onViewChanged, SeedRetryTask for proactive re-contact during quorum loss, and ViewReconciler (higher-incarnation-wins; severity DEAD > SUSPECTED > ALIVE on ties) wired into RapidMembership.handleViewChangeProposal.

obligations.description: F04.R47-R50 (AMENDED): rebalancing is immediate on view change. ClusteredEngine.onViewChanged invalidates the RendezvousOwnership cache and the next query recomputes. GracePeriodManager tracks departures/returns but does not gate rebalancing. Satisfying R47-R50 requires: hold a departed member's partitions unowned for gracePeriod before redistribution; on expiration redistribute only the departed member's partitions without moving partitions among still-live members (differential HRW or stable-ownership-with-deltas); on rejoin within grace cancel the pending rebalance and reclaim previous assignments.
obligations.blocked_by: grace-gated rebalance scheduler + differential ownership scheme
obligations.resolved_by: f04-obligation-resolution WD-05: GraceGatedRebalancer schedules deferred rebalance at grace expiry; RendezvousOwnership.differentialAssign recomputes only affected partition IDs so still-live members keep their assignments; cancelPending(NodeAddress) aborts a pending rebalance when a node rejoins within grace.

obligations.description: F04.R63 (AMENDED): ClusteredTable.scan scatters to every live member. Predicate-driven partition pruning using the partition-key predicate to narrow fanout to intersecting owners is not implemented. Blocks on having a concrete partition-key mapping surfaced through RendezvousOwnership so the client can ask 'which owners intersect [fromKey, toKey]' without a full scan.
obligations.resolved_by: f04-obligation-resolution WD-05: PartitionKeySpace SPI (SinglePartitionKeySpace fallback + LexicographicPartitionKeySpace range-based impl), RendezvousOwnership.ownersForKeyRange resolves intersecting partition owners, ClusteredTable.scan delegates owner resolution to the configured keyspace so fanout narrows to range-overlapping owners instead of every live member.

obligations.description: F04.R56/R57/R79 (CODE BUG NOT REPAIRED INLINE): ClusteredEngine.Builder does not accept a DiscoveryProvider as a mandatory parameter and ClusteredEngine does not orchestrate the join sequence (discovery.register + membership.start). Callers must start the membership protocol externally before passing it to the builder. Fix requires expanding the builder signature, ordering the join sequence with rollback on partial failure, and updating tests/examples. Not repaired inline because it alters the builder public API and surrounding fixtures.
obligations.blocked_by: ClusteredEngine.Builder public API expansion + lifecycle orchestration
obligations.resolved_by: f04-obligation-resolution WD-02: ClusteredEngine.Builder.discovery(...) mandatory; ClusteredEngine.join(seeds) orchestrates discovery.register -> membership.start with rollback on partial failure; close() deregisters symmetrically (R58 bonus)

obligations.description: F04.R60 (CODE BUG NOT REPAIRED INLINE): ClusteredTable always routes CRUD through RemotePartitionClient even when the resolved owner equals localAddress. A local short-circuit (owner.equals(localAddress) -> invoke localEngine.getTable(name) directly) is required. Not applied inline because ClusteredTable does not hold a reference to the local Engine today; wiring it requires a constructor change propagated through ClusteredEngine.createTable.
obligations.blocked_by: ClusteredTable constructor now needs a local-engine reference
obligations.resolved_by: f04-obligation-resolution WD-02: ClusteredTable 6-arg constructor with Engine localEngine; CRUD and scan route directly through localEngine.getTable(name) when owner.equals(localAddress)

obligations.description: F04.R68 (CODE BUG NOT REPAIRED INLINE): RemotePartitionClient payloads include [opcode][key][doc] but not the target table name or partition identifier. A remote node receiving QUERY_REQUEST cannot route to the correct table. Payload format needs a table-name prefix and partition-id field. Not repaired inline because the remote-side QUERY_REQUEST dispatcher has not been written end-to-end yet, so a format change has no downstream consumer to validate against today.
obligations.resolved_by: WD-03: new QueryRequestPayload [tableNameLen][tableName][partitionId][opcode][body] format + QueryRequestHandler dispatcher registered on transport by ClusteredEngine

obligations.description: F04.R77 (CODE BUG NOT REPAIRED INLINE): ClusteredTable.scan issues partition requests sequentially; each getRange blocks in sendRequestAndAwait before the next partition is contacted. Parallel fanout via transport.request futures plus a barrier-style collection is required. Not repaired inline because getRange eager-materializes the entire partition response into a List — a correct streaming parallel implementation needs getRange to return a CompletableFuture<Iterator<TableEntry<String>>> first, a broader refactor than a spec-verify session.
obligations.resolved_by: WD-03: PartitionClient.getRangeAsync default + RemotePartitionClient.getRangeAsync transport.request override + ClusteredTable.scan parallel fanout via virtual-thread scatter executor and CompletableFuture.allOf

obligations.description: F04.R53 (PARTIAL, documented): GracePeriodManager accepts an injected Clock and all operations capture a single now per call (R95), so intra-call jitter does not produce inconsistent results. However the default (Clock.systemUTC()) is wall-clock, not monotonic; a backward clock adjustment between calls can extend the grace window (demonstrated by SharedStateAdversarialTest.test_GracePeriodManager_isInGracePeriod_clockBackwardJumpExtendsGracePeriod). Fix requires a MonotonicClock on System.nanoTime() with a fixed Instant epoch, or migrating the manager to long-nanos internally. Not repaired inline because it touches every Instant/Duration API surface and all callers.
obligations.blocked_by: MonotonicClock design or nano-based API refactor

obligations.description: F04.R10 (AMENDED): amendment clarifies the base membership protocol does not today deduplicate received messages by sender sequence number because view-change application is already idempotent under R91/R92. Future replication layers requiring exactly-once semantics will need per-sender lastSeenSequence tracking on each receiver; Message.sequenceNumber is in place for that use.
obligations.resolved_by: f04-obligation-resolution WD-01: MonotonicClock wrapping System.nanoTime() with fixed Instant epoch; GracePeriodManager default constructor now uses MonotonicClock

obligations.description: F04.R20 (AMENDED): PhiAccrualFailureDetector does not initialize its window with protocolPeriod; it relies on the >=2-heartbeat floor (phi returns 0.0 until two samples) and RapidMembership seeds an initial heartbeat on first tick when phi==0. This matches the Cassandra-style implementation and the jlsm test behavior but drifts from the classical description that expects a seeded mean. If startup false positives appear under a high protocolPeriod, inject protocolPeriod as a synthetic first interval.

F04.R29 (AMENDED): amendment moves request-timeout enforcement to the client layer (RemotePartitionClient.timeoutMs) since the InJvmTransport has no blocking send path to interrupt. A future network-backed transport should own timeout enforcement at the transport boundary so calls made via ClusterTransport.request directly (not through RemotePartitionClient) get the same guarantee.

F04.R39 (NOT REPAIRED INLINE): listener callbacks are invoked synchronously on the membership protocol thread outside viewLock. A slow listener blocks subsequent message processing even though it does not block view mutation. The spec requires listener notification be fully asynchronous so a slow listener cannot delay protocol progress. Fix requires a dedicated listener executor (bounded, with drop or backpressure policy on overflow) plus tests covering slow-listener and throwing-listener cases.
obligations.resolved_by: f04-obligation-resolution WD-01: ListenerDispatcher<L> with bounded single-thread executor, drop-oldest overflow, per-listener exception isolation; RapidMembership.notify* routed through dispatcher

obligations.description: F05.R37 AMENDED: Table.query() throws UnsupportedOperationException because TableQuery has a private constructor and cannot be instantiated outside jlsm.table. The amended spec permits UOE until the query-binding facility lands in F10. Resolution requires (a) adding TableQuery<String> query() to jlsm.table.JlsmTable.StringKeyed (cross-module API change), (b) refactoring StringKeyedTable to carry schema + IndexRegistry, (c) making TableQuery bindable (package-private constructor) and (d) wiring execute() to QueryExecutor. Blocked on F10 query infrastructure work.
obligations.resolved_by: cross-module-integration WD-03: TableQuery bindable via QueryRunner factory + StringKeyedTable.query() + QueryExecutor wiring

obligations.description: Deprecation window for the 4-arg and 5-arg `CatalogClusteredTable.forEngine` overloads must close. Resolution is either (a) remove the overloads after all callers migrate to the canonical entry point with an explicit local engine handle, or (b) re-affirm the overloads with a new requirement defining transport-only dispatch semantics that replaces the audited silent-fallthrough behavior.
obligations.blocked_by: test-suite migration (ClusteredTableTest, ContractBoundariesAdversarialTest, EngineClusteringAdversarialTest, ResourceLifecycleAdversarialTest, SharedStateAdversarialTest, ConcurrencyAdversarialTest, ClusteredTableScanParallelTest, ClusteredTableScanPruningTest, ClusteredTableLocalShortCircuitTest)
